content.text: Claude starter templates evolve over time: new Claude model defaults, updated CI gate logic, improved prompt structures, and additional scaffold directories.
and tracking the generating commit in each project's
makes it possible to safely upgrade existing projects to newer template releases — without overwriting local modifications.

attrs.alt: Template versioning lifecycle: copy from tag, then update with three-way merge

content.text: When Copier generates a project, it writes a
file containing every answered variable and a
field recording the exact git commit (or tag) of the template used. This file is the anchor for future

# .copier-answers.yml — generated automatically, commit it to version control
_commit: v1.1.0
_src_path: ssh://git@github.com/your-org/claude-service-template.git
service_name: invoice-extractor
team_name: finance-platform
claude_model: claude-sonnet-5
max_tokens: 2048
runtime: python-container
enable_evals: true
enable_idp_catalog: true
Always commit .copier-answers.yml. It is the contract between your project and the template: it records what version you were generated from and what variable values were used.
Template repositories should follow semantic versioning to communicate the risk level of each release clearly to consuming teams.
Safe to update immediately — no structural changes
Fix typo in README, update claude_model default, fix hook bug
New files or optional sections added — existing files unchanged
Add evals/ directory, add optional IDP catalog step, new CI job
Breaking change — file renamed, variable removed, restructure
handler.py → service.py, remove legacy prompt format, new required variable

# In the template repository: create a release tag
git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "feat: add architecture fitness function CI gate"
git push origin v1.2.0

# In a consuming project: update to a specific minor release
copier update --vcs-ref v1.2.0

# Update to the latest tag on the template default branch
copier update

# Preview what will change before applying
copier update --pretend --vcs-ref v1.2.0
for every file the template owns:
: the file as rendered at the previously-recorded
: the file as rendered at the new target version
: the file as it currently exists in the project
Copier applies the diff between Base and Theirs onto Ours. Lines you never touched merge cleanly. Lines where you and the template both changed trigger a conflict marker — the same format as

<<<<<<< before updating
# Old template default — do not edit
MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
=======
# Updated template default — do not edit
MODEL = "claude-sonnet-5"
>>>>>>> after updating

Conflict markers in files the template owns usually mean you modified a section that the template also changed. Resolve by accepting the new template version (theirs) and moving your customization to a non-template file that
Major bumps rename or restructure files. Copier cannot rename files automatically — it can only add new files and update existing ones in place. A breaking rename (

# hooks/pre_update.py — runs before copier re-renders on 'copier update'
import pathlib, sys, shutil

OLD_HANDLER = pathlib.Path("handler.py")
NEW_HANDLER = pathlib.Path("service.py")

# Detect v1→v2 migration need
if OLD_HANDLER.exists() and not NEW_HANDLER.exists():
    print("[pre-update] Migrating handler.py → service.py for v2.x compatibility")
    shutil.copy(OLD_HANDLER, NEW_HANDLER)
    OLD_HANDLER.unlink()           # copier will now write the new template version to service.py
    print("[pre-update] Migration complete. Resolve any merge conflicts in service.py.")
With the pre-update hook in place, teams can run
and the rename is handled automatically. Post-update hooks can clean up residual artifacts.
There are two common policies for how consuming projects track the template version:
Safe: teams control when they upgrade. Risk: divergence grows; security fixes require manual campaign
Update CI runs copier update weekly; PR opened if diff is non-empty
Always current, low drift. Risk: minor breaking changes reach all teams before they opt in
Automated PRs for patch/minor; teams manually approve major
Balance: routine improvements land automatically; breaking changes need explicit review
For enterprise teams, minor-pinned is the recommended default: automate patch and minor updates, require explicit opt-in for major version bumps.
A dedicated CI workflow in the
can open pull requests in all consuming repositories when a new version is released:

# .github/workflows/propagate-updates.yml  (in the TEMPLATE repo)
name: Propagate template updates

on:
  push:
    tags: ["v*"]

jobs:
  update-consumers:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        # Enumerate consuming repos — or read from a registry
        repo:
          - your-org/invoice-extractor
          - your-org/doc-classifier
          - your-org/contract-reviewer

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ matrix.repo }}
          token: ${{ secrets.TEMPLATE_BOT_TOKEN }}

      - name: Install copier
        run: pip install copier

      - name: Run copier update
        run: copier update --vcs-ref ${{ github.ref_name }} --defaults --overwrite

      - name: Open PR if changes present
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6
        with:
          title: "chore: update from Claude template ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          body: |
            Automated update from the Claude starter template.
            Review the diff and resolve any conflicts before merging.
          branch: "template-update/${{ github.ref_name }}"
          commit-message: "chore: apply Claude template ${{ github.ref_name }}"
Consuming projects have responsibilities that make upgrades reliable:
: put customizations in companion files excluded from template management (add them to
cannot determine the base revision and will fail
Run copier update in a branch
: never update directly on the main branch; review the diff in a PR before merging
must point to the canonical template repository, not a fork
in the branch before merging the update PR
Every template release should ship a
entry and, for major bumps, a
guide. These travel with the template repo and are consulted by teams running

# CHANGELOG.md (in template repo)

## [2.0.0] — 2025-09-01
### Breaking Changes
- `handler.py` renamed to `service.py`. Run `copier update --vcs-ref v2.0.0`;
  the pre_update hook handles the rename automatically.
- Variable `max_tokens` default changed from 2048 to 4096.
  Review your cost budgets before merging the update PR.
- CI gate 1 now requires `pytest --cov` with 80 % minimum coverage.
  Add missing tests before merging.

### New Features
- Architecture fitness function CI gate added (gate 5).
- IDP catalog auto-registration now uses Backstage v1.30 API.

### Migration Guide
See docs/migration/v2.md for step-by-step upgrade instructions.

## [1.1.0] — 2025-06-15
### New Features
- Optional `evals/` directory for seed eval cases.
- `enable_idp_catalog` variable added (default: true).
When both the template and the project modify the same region of a file, Copier emits standard git-style conflict markers. The correct resolution strategy depends on
Developer edited a region marked 'do not edit'
Accept theirs: copy the new template version, move local logic to a companion file
Both sides add the same variable name
Developer and template independently added the same config key
Accept theirs (template version) — check the value matches your intent
Local auto-formatter changed whitespace in a template file
Add the file to your formatter's ignore list, accept theirs
Developer added a function inside a template-managed file
Move to a companion file; accept theirs for the template sections

# After copier update — find all conflict markers
grep -rn "<<<<<<< before" . --include="*.py" --include="*.yml" --include="*.yaml"

# Resolve a specific file conflict by accepting template version
git checkout --theirs -- handler.py
git add handler.py

# OR: open in your editor and resolve manually, then stage
code handler.py
git add handler.py

# After resolving all conflicts, commit
git commit -m "chore: resolve copier update conflicts for v1.2.0"
Across a fleet of dozens of services, version drift is inevitable unless actively monitored. A scheduled CI job can scan all service repositories, read their
, and report which ones are behind the latest template tag.

# scripts/check_template_drift.py
# Run in a CI job that has access to all consuming repos
import subprocess, yaml, json, sys
from pathlib import Path

TEMPLATE_REPO = "your-org/claude-service-template"
CONSUMER_REPOS = [
    "your-org/invoice-extractor",
    "your-org/doc-classifier",
    "your-org/contract-reviewer",
]

# Get the latest template tag
latest_tag = subprocess.check_output(
    ["gh", "api", f"repos/{TEMPLATE_REPO}/releases/latest", "--jq", ".tag_name"],
    text=True,
).strip()

drift_report = []
for repo in CONSUMER_REPOS:
    answers_raw = subprocess.check_output(
        ["gh", "api", f"repos/{repo}/contents/.copier-answers.yml",
         "--jq", ".content"],
        text=True,
    )
    import base64
    answers = yaml.safe_load(base64.b64decode(answers_raw).decode())
    current = answers.get("_commit", "unknown")
    if current != latest_tag:
        drift_report.append({"repo": repo, "current": current, "latest": latest_tag})

if drift_report:
    print(f"\n{'Repo':<40} {'Current':<12} {'Latest':<12}")
    print("-" * 65)
    for d in drift_report:
        print(f"{d['repo']:<40} {d['current']:<12} {d['latest']:<12}")
    sys.exit(1)   # Fail CI to alert the platform team
else:
    print(f"All {len(CONSUMER_REPOS)} services on latest template {latest_tag}")
Testing Upgrade Paths in the Template Repository

The template repository should include upgrade integration tests that verify a project generated from an older tag can successfully receive an update to the new tag. These tests catch regressions before consumers are affected.

# tests/test_upgrade_path.py (in the TEMPLATE repo)
import copier, pathlib, subprocess, pytest

# Pairs of (from_tag, to_tag) to test upgrading between
UPGRADE_PATHS = [
    ("v1.0.0", "v1.1.0"),
    ("v1.1.0", "v2.0.0"),   # major: tests that migration hook runs cleanly
]

TEMPLATE_ROOT = str(pathlib.Path(__file__).parent.parent)
DATA = {
    "service_name": "test-service",
    "team_name": "platform",
    "claude_model": "claude-sonnet-5",
    "runtime": "python-container",
    "enable_evals": True,
    "enable_idp_catalog": False,
}


@pytest.mark.parametrize("from_tag,to_tag", UPGRADE_PATHS)
def test_upgrade_path(from_tag, to_tag, tmp_path):
    # Step 1: generate from the older tag
    copier.run_copy(
        src_path=TEMPLATE_ROOT,
        dst_path=str(tmp_path),
        vcs_ref=from_tag,
        data=DATA,
        defaults=True,
        overwrite=True,
        quiet=True,
        unsafe=True,
    )
    assert (tmp_path / ".copier-answers.yml").exists()

    # Step 2: update to the newer tag
    copier.run_update(
        dst_path=str(tmp_path),
        vcs_ref=to_tag,
        defaults=True,
        overwrite=True,
        quiet=True,
        unsafe=True,
        conflict="inline",   # embed markers rather than aborting
    )

    # Step 3: no conflict markers should remain (hooks must have resolved them)
    conflicts = list(tmp_path.rglob("*.py")) + list(tmp_path.rglob("*.yml"))
    for f in conflicts:
        content = f.read_text(errors="ignore")
        assert "<<<<<<< before" not in content, \
            f"Unresolved conflict in {f} after upgrading {from_tag}→{to_tag}"
Operational Runbook: Releasing a New Template Version
This runbook describes the end-to-end steps for releasing a new minor or major template version and propagating it to consuming teams.
in a branch off the template repo's
. Add migration hooks for breaking changes.
with the version number, a summary of changes, and any migration instructions.
git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m '<release summary>' && git push origin vX.Y.Z
workflow opens update PRs in all consuming repos within minutes of the tag push.
via your internal developer portal announcement channel with a link to the migration guide and a deadline for teams to merge their update PRs.
: 7 days after the tag, run the drift detection script. Follow up with any teams still behind.

For major version releases, give teams a minimum two-week window between the tag and any deprecation of the old tag. Services have review cycles; an aggressive deadline creates technical debt pressure that leads to un-reviewed merges.
Copier tags the template repository at each release; consuming projects record the exact
Semantic versioning (patch/minor/major) communicates upgrade risk: patch and minor are safe to automate; major bumps signal breaking changes that require human review.
applies a three-way merge: template-to-template diff applied onto the project's current state, preserving local edits in non-template files.
Major renames and restructures need explicit migration hooks (
) since Copier cannot rename files automatically.

A CI propagation workflow in the template repository can open update PRs in all consuming repositories automatically when a new tag is pushed — enabling fleet-wide template freshness with minimal manual effort.
